audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for sons of God/daughters of human language, Nephilim, human corruption, divine grief, Noah righteousness, covenant, and ark terminology. Final source-critical review may refine difficult ancient terms.

verses.restored: When the human began to multiply on the face of the ground, and daughters were born to them,

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them,

notes.title: The human
notes.body: Ha-adam can mean humanity collectively. The scene concerns human multiplication before the flood.

verses.restored: the sons of the Divine saw that the daughters of the human were good, and they took women for themselves, from all whom they chose.

familiar.text: That the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they were fair; and they took them wives of all which they chose.

notes.title: Sons of the Divine
notes.body: Bene ha-elohim is difficult. It may refer to divine beings, heavenly council figures, or powerful beings. The translation preserves the strangeness instead of reducing it too quickly.

notes.title: Took whom they chose
notes.body: The taking language carries coercive overtones and anticipates the chapter's theme of corrupted power.

verses.restored: And the Eternal said, My spirit shall not contend with the human forever, for he is flesh; his days shall be one hundred twenty years.

familiar.text: And the LORD said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also is flesh: yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.

notes.title: Spirit and flesh
notes.body: Ruach and basar appear in tension here. Flesh is mortality and embodied limitation, not inherently evil matter.

notes.title: One hundred twenty years
notes.body: This may refer to a lifespan limit or a period before judgment; the text is compact.

verses.restored: The Nephilim were on the earth in those days, and also afterward, when the sons of the Divine came in to the daughters of the human and they bore children to them. They were the mighty ones of old, people of the name.

familiar.text: There were giants in the earth in those days; and also after that, when the sons of God came in unto the daughters of men, and they bare children to them, the same became mighty men which were of old, men of renown.

notes.title: Nephilim
notes.body: Nephilim is preserved because giants is an interpretation. The word is ancient and debated.

notes.title: People of the name
notes.body: Anshe ha-shem means men/people of name or renown. It suggests fame, reputation, and remembered power.

verses.restored: And the Eternal saw that the harm of the human was great on the earth, and that every shaping of the thoughts of his heart was only harm all the day.

familiar.text: And GOD saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.

notes.title: Shaping of thoughts
notes.body: Yetser points to formation, shaping, or inclination. The issue is not one bad act but the inner patterning of the heart.

notes.title: Harm
notes.body: Ra is rendered harm here to emphasize destructive orientation rather than abstract evil alone.

verses.restored: And the Eternal regretted that he had made the human on the earth, and he was grieved to his heart.

familiar.text: And it repented the LORD that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him at his heart.

notes.title: Divine grief
notes.body: The Hebrew presents divine sorrow and grief, not cold detachment. The violence of creation wounds the Divine heart.

verses.restored: And the Eternal said, I will wipe away the human whom I created from the face of the ground, from human to animal, creeping thing, and winged creature of the heavens, for I regret that I made them.

familiar.text: And the LORD said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and creeping thing, and fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.

notes.title: Wipe away
notes.body: Machah means wipe out or blot out. The judgment language is severe and should not be softened.

verses.restored: But Noah found favor in the eyes of the Eternal.

familiar.text: But Noah found grace in the eyes of the LORD.

notes.title: Favor
notes.body: Chen means favor or grace. Noah becomes the preserved line within judgment.

verses.restored: These are the generations of Noah. Noah was a righteous man, complete in his generations. Noah walked with the Divine.

familiar.text: These are the generations of Noah: Noah was a just man and perfect in his generations, and Noah walked with God.

notes.title: Righteous and complete
notes.body: Tsaddiq means righteous or just; tamim means complete, whole, blameless, or sound. These terms mark Noah as aligned within a corrupt generation.

notes.title: Walked with the Divine
notes.body: The phrase echoes Enoch in Genesis 5.

verses.restored: Noah fathered three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

familiar.text: And Noah begat three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

verses.restored: The earth was ruined before the Divine, and the earth was filled with violence.

familiar.text: The earth also was corrupt before God, and the earth was filled with violence.

notes.title: Ruined/corrupt
notes.body: Shachat can mean ruin, corrupt, destroy, or spoil. The earth is both corrupted and about to be destroyed with the same root in the chapter.

notes.title: Violence
notes.body: Chamas means violence, wrong, or violent injustice. This is the named condition filling the earth.

verses.restored: And the Divine saw the earth, and look: it was ruined, for all flesh had ruined its way upon the earth.

familiar.text: And God looked upon the earth, and, behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted his way upon the earth.

notes.title: All flesh
notes.body: Basar includes living embodied creatures. The corruption is broad, not merely private inward sin.

verses.restored: And the Divine said to Noah, The end of all flesh has come before me, for the earth is filled with violence through them. Look, I am about to ruin them with the earth.

familiar.text: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.

notes.title: Ruin with the earth
notes.body: The same root shachat links human corruption and divine destruction. The destroyer act mirrors the ruined condition.

verses.restored: Make for yourself an ark of gopher wood. You shall make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and outside with pitch.

familiar.text: Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.

notes.title: Ark
notes.body: Tevah is also used for the basket of Moses. It is a vessel of preservation through waters.

notes.title: Gopher wood
notes.body: The exact wood is uncertain; gopher is transliterated.

verses.restored: This is how you shall make it: the length of the ark three hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.

familiar.text: And this is the fashion which thou shalt make it of: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirty cubits.

verses.restored: You shall make a window for the ark, and finish it to a cubit from above. Set the door of the ark in its side. Make it with lower, second, and third levels.

familiar.text: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.

notes.title: Window/opening
notes.body: The word tsohar is difficult; it may mean roof, window, or light-opening.

verses.restored: And I, look, I am bringing the flood of waters upon the earth, to ruin all flesh under the heavens in which is the breath of life. Everything that is on the earth shall expire.

familiar.text: And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; and every thing that is in the earth shall die.

notes.title: Flood
notes.body: Mabbul is the flood term used for this event. It is not the ordinary word for any flood.

notes.title: Breath of life
notes.body: The judgment is against embodied life under heaven, emphasizing the scale of the catastrophe.

verses.restored: But I will establish my covenant with you. You shall come into the ark: you, your sons, your woman, and your sons’ women with you.

familiar.text: But with thee will I establish my covenant; and thou shalt come into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons' wives with thee.

notes.title: Covenant
notes.body: Berit appears here as the promise structure of preservation before the flood arrives.

verses.restored: And from every living thing of all flesh, two of each you shall bring into the ark to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female.

familiar.text: And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep them alive with thee; they shall be male and female.

verses.restored: From the winged creatures according to their kind, from the animals according to their kind, and from every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind, two of each shall come to you to keep them alive.

familiar.text: Of fowls after their kind, and of cattle after their kind, of every creeping thing of the earth after his kind, two of every sort shall come unto thee, to keep them alive.

verses.restored: And you, take for yourself from every food that is eaten, and gather it to yourself. It shall be food for you and for them.

familiar.text: And take thou unto thee of all food that is eaten, and thou shalt gather it to thee; and it shall be for food for thee, and for them.

verses.restored: And Noah did so. According to all that the Divine commanded him, so he did.

familiar.text: Thus did Noah; according to all that God commanded him, so did he.

notes.title: Noah did so
notes.body: The chapter ends with obedience matching command.
